program.name: QBE Graduate Program
program.rotation_text: QBE says graduates spend the first week together in an induction week, and then start in their first rotation team from week two.

eligibility.work_rights: You need proof of unrestricted Australian working rights.
eligibility.graduation_window: You need a bachelor’s degree completed within two years before the program commencement date. QBE also accepts postgraduate qualifications completed within two years of the program commencement date.
minimum_requirements: Submit a resume and your current academic transcript.
Provide proof of unrestricted Australian working rights.

stages.name: Recruitment timeline
stages.details: Application in July, assessment in August, and selection in September.

stages.name: Online application
stages.details: Submit your resume and current academic transcript. QBE says a cover letter is not required for its graduate program.

stages.name: Online experience
stages.details: Complete ability, working style and personality assessments, and an activity exploring QBE and its values.

stages.name: Phone interview
stages.details: Interview with a member of the graduate recruitment team.

stages.name: Final assessment centre
stages.details: Group activity and an individual interview. For technical program streams, there may also be a technical interview or a case study.

stages.name: Probity checks and offer
stages.details: Probity checks, then an offer to join the QBE graduate program.

tips: QBE says the application process can vary between program streams.
QBE says a cover letter is not required for its graduate program.
QBE suggests researching the program streams before choosing which one to apply for.

exec_themes: Disciplined underwriting to deliver consistent insurance profits (focus on pricing, risk selection, and keeping the combined operating ratio strong).
Executing a medium-term strategy and operational modernisation initiatives to improve efficiency and long-term performance.
Managing catastrophe and climate-related risk as a core part of the business (including governance, risk frameworks, and climate-related disclosures).
Supporting the transition to a lower-carbon economy through actions in underwriting and investments, alongside net zero operations goals.
Community resilience and inclusion initiatives via the QBE Foundation, including support for climate-vulnerable communities.
commercial_context.profit_engine: QBE’s core profit engine is earning underwriting profits on insurance premiums (measured with metrics like the combined operating ratio and insurance profit margin), supported by investment income on its investment portfolio.
commercial_context.headwinds: Catastrophe activity and natural disaster losses remain an ongoing risk driver. Competitive conditions can make growth harder in some markets/lines. Claims inflation is also a pressure, although QBE noted it was gradually easing in 1H25.
commercial_context.esg: QBE positions climate change and natural disasters as top sustainability topics and uses an Environmental and Social Risk Framework for underwriting/investments. It has targets/commitments including net zero operations by 2030 (Scope 1, 2 and selected Scope 3), sourcing 100% renewable electricity by end-2025 (RE100 commitment), and continued climate-related reporting (including climate-related financial disclosure aligned to AASB S2 from February 2026, per QBE’s sustainability site). QBE also highlights community-focused work through the QBE Foundation (e.g., partnership supporting climate-vulnerable communities).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: QBE says it has reassessed its prior commitment to achieve net zero emissions in its underwriting and investment portfolios by 2050, moving to a revised approach focused on actions ‘within QBE’s control’ while maintaining net zero operations by 2030. It also announced in March 2025 that it resigned from the Net-Zero Asset Owners Alliance and will incorporate its ‘net zero investments by 2050’ approach into a broader climate transition plan to be released in 2026.

notes: Program name and streams were taken from the QBE Graduate Program page.
Rotation timing (induction week and starting the first rotation from week two) was taken from the QBE Graduate Program page.
No explicit salary, duration, rotation length, or city/state locations were stated on the extracted pages.

sources.title: QBE Insurance Graduate Program | QBE Careers | QBE AU

notes: Work rights and application document requirements were taken from the FAQs on the Application process page.
The graduation window was taken from the 'What level of education is required?' FAQ on the Application process page.

Disciplines were not explicitly listed as formal eligibility requirements on the extracted pages (QBE notes some technical streams may require a particular field of study, and to see each stream job ad for details).

sources.title: Application process | QBE Graduate Program | QBE AU

notes: Recruitment timeline and the step-by-step process were taken from the Application process page.
QBE states applications are closed for the 2026 graduate program, but the page does not state specific opening or closing dates.

sources.title: Application process | QBE Graduate Program | QBE AU

notes: Some items (especially the ‘profit_engine’ and ‘headwinds’) are synthesized in plain English from performance/strategy language in QBE’s official investor materials; QBE does not present a single labeled ‘profit engine’ section.

The phrase ‘operational modernisation initiatives’ and the idea of a ‘medium-term strategy’ are taken from investor reporting language; details on specific programs are not fully enumerated in the extracted snippets.

The ‘recent_pivot’ reflects QBE’s stated reassessment of its net zero underwriting/investments commitment and membership change (NZAOA). The practical implications (exact target changes and milestones) are not fully specified on the cited pages.
